data.title: Solana Price Craters 40% From January High as Meme Coin Bubble Bursts
data.description:
Solana (SOL) has been downtrending and retracing heavily over the past month. The asset has declined 40% since its Jan. 19 all-time high of $293, and the trend seems set to continue as meme coin mania fades. Moreover, SOL is down a further 8% on the day, falling below $170 for the first time since early November.

Solana’s popularity and high usage in recent months have been primarily driven by meme coin minting and speculation, but that hasstarted falling.

Degens have flocked to the low-cost blockchain to mint and shill meme coins on platforms likePump.fun. The vast majority of these spurious tokens have no utility and serve no purpose, and a high percentage were pump-and-dump schemes.Game Over For Solana?

“It’s over for Solana,” lamented crypto trader ‘Xremlin’ who added it is “Worse than the FTX collapse.”

The trader blamed theMELANIAtoken, the namesake coin of the First Lady launched just days after TRUMP, and LIBRA, a token allegedly promoted by Argentina’s president Javier Milei, which he has denied.

Insider trading caused pump and dumps liquidating retail traders, adding to the fall of Solana, the analyst said.

“This past month showed how bad things are on Solana’s meme scene. Mostly, it’s insiders making money while everyone else loses. Today, the SOL price began responding to this sentiment.”

It’s over for Solana

Worse than the FTX collapse$LIBRA, #MELANIA changed everything

: Here’s what went down and what’s next… pic.twitter.com/mo6TMBpift

— Xremlin (@0x_gremlin) February 17, 2025

Meanwhile, crypto investor “Ash Crypto” said we now know the real reason that Solana meme coins started dying. Insiders have been sniping these meme coin launches and selling to dump on retail, he said.

“Insiders have extracted $200+ Million from these Solana launches so far.”

“Solana and SOL memes are dumping hard because people are realizing this whole game was rigged, and it was all just wealth transfer/value extraction from crypto gamblers to project insiders,” he said in a separate post.

Others posted some shocking statistics about how much has been extracted from Solana meme coins:

calculated the total extracted from memes on solana: – trading bots & apps: 1.09bn – pumpfun: 492m – MEV: 1.5-2bn – Trump insiders: 0.5-1bn – Other insiders: unknown – AMMs: 0-2bn

Total: 3.6-6.6+ bn

Sources in the next tweet

— 0xngmi (@0xngmi) February 15, 2025

Ethereum Makes Surprise Move

As if in response to the SOL slump, ETH prices have bucked the market trend, topping $2,830 in a 12-day high on Monday.

However, market momentum was too much for the asset to overcome and ETH fell back below $2,700 again during trading in Asia on Tuesday.

Total market cap is down 3% on the day at $3.27 trillion and SOL is the largest loser in the top 40 crypto assets – while Solana-based meme coins are not far behind.

data.title: Capgemini Net Profit Rises Despite Lower Revenue Amid Challenging Environment
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Capgemini reported higher profit for the last year despite lower revenue, reflecting the group's resilience amid a challenging environment.

The French consulting and technology group posted a 0.5% rise in net profit to 1.67 billion euros, equivalent to $1.75 billion, from 1.66 billion euros for the same period a year earlier. This missed market consensus of 1.69 billion euros based on 13 analysts taken from FactSet.

Operating profit increased to 2.36 billion euros from 2.35 billion euros, while operating margin fell 1.9% on year.

Revenue fell to 22.10 billion euros from 22.52 billion euros, ahead of market consensus of 22.05 billion based on 16 analysts taken from FactSet.

Within this, revenue in North America decreased 4.1%, while U.K. and Ireland region posted a 1.0% decline reflecting the contraction of the consumer goods and retail sector. Meanwhile, France revenue fell 3.5% and the Rest of Europe region revenue grew 0.1%, with solid public and energy and utilities sectors and financial services returning to growth.

The group declared a final dividend of 3.40 euros a share, flat from a year earlier.

Looking ahead, Capgemini expects constant-currency revenue growth of minus 2.0% to 2.0%, with an operating margin between 13.3% to 13.5% for 2025. It also anticipates organic free cash flow of around 1.9 billion euros.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: China Eastern Airlines' Passenger Count, Freight Volume Up in January
data.description:
China Eastern Airlines' passenger count rose 12% to 12.7 million in January from 11.3 million a year ago.

Freight carried inched up 0.8% to 97,490 tonnes from 96,730 tonnes. International freight grew 7% to 35,300 tonnes, according to a Monday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

Passenger capacity rose 12% to 27.8 billion available seat kilometers in January from 24.9 billion available seat kilometers a year earlier.

Cargo capacity increased 12% to 978.1 million available freight ton-kilometers from 875.3 million available freight ton-kilometers, the filing said.

Shares slid 1% in Shanghai during Tuesday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Rapid Lithium Raises AU$1 Million Via Shortfall Placement
data.description:
Rapid Lithium received firm commitments to place the full amount of its entitlement issue shortfall of AU$1 million, comprising 250 million shares at AU$0.004 per share, according to a Tuesday Australian bourse filing.

The shortfall placement also includes a three-for-two free attaching option, exercisable at a price AU$0.017 per option expiring Oct. 23, 2027, with the issue of 375 million options.

Strata Holdings, one of the firm's largest shareholders, decided to participate in the placement, subject to shareholder approval.

The settlement of the shortfall shares and options is anticipated for Feb. 24.

data.title: Edenred beats annual profit expectations, driven by medium-term strategic plan
data.description:
French vouchers provider Edenred reported higher-than-expected annual core profit on Tuesday, beating a company-provided consensus, helped by the rollout of its medium-term "Beyond22-25" strategic plan.

The group, known for its "Ticket Restaurant" vouchers, carried out a series of acquisitions in 2024, notably Spirii, IP, and RB, to seize external opportunities to cushion any deterioration in the economic environment.

High inflation and interest rates have boosted benefits providers like Edenred and Pluxee , as employers sought ways to support staff without raising wages, but the recent easing of interest rates could pose a threat to sustaining this growth.

The group reported a 19% organic rise in earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) to 1.27 billion euros ($1.33 billion) in 2024, above a company-provided consensus of 1.26 billion euros.

"For 2025, despite an uncertain economic environment in Europe, we have set ourselves the ambitious target of growing our EBITDA by more than 10% on a like-for-like basis," said Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Bertrand Dumazy.

Edenred confirmed its 2025 guidance, saying it had priced in a negative EBITDA impact of 60 million euros from a cap on merchant commissions in Italy.

It announced its highest earnings per share to date of 2.07 euros for 2024, up 21% from a year ago.

($1 = 0.9565 euros)

data.title: Nikkei Rises 0.2%, Led by Financial, Heavy-Industry Stocks — Market Talk
data.description:
0654 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher as hopes continued for further Bank of Japan rate increases on solid economic growth. Financial and heavy-industry stocks led the gains. Resona Holdings rose 3.6%, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group climbed 2.0% and IHI advanced 6.3%. The Nikkei Stock Average edged 0.2% higher to 39270.40. The newest 10-year Japanese government bond yield increased 4.5 basis points to 1.430%, the highest level since November 2009. Investors are focusing on U.S. trade and foreign policies as the earnings season winds down in Japan. USD/JPY was at 151.96, compared with 151.50 as of Monday 5 p.m. ET. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0647 GMT - Growing fiscal concerns have the potential to drive U.S. Treasury yields higher, DZ Bank Research's Birgit Henseler says in a note. The trend for higher yields over a 12-month period could also be strengthened by the debate about tax breaks, the analyst says. "By the autumn at the latest, the debate about tax cuts and an associated expansion of the budget deficit could therefore gain momentum," she says. DZ Bank Research forecasts the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.50% in three months, which is largely in line with the current level. DZ Bank Research expects the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.70% in six months and at 5.00% both at the end of the year and also in 12 months. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0631 GMT - The evolving security situation in Europe is likely to impact bond market dynamics, Nordea analysts Anders Skytte Aalund and Christian Peter Kjaer say in a note. Finland is one of the European countries with a strong military force, so despite an expectation of further military investments, Finland's starting point is strong relative to other European countries, they say. The prospect of a peace deal for Ukraine could mean higher defense investments in Europe, potentially leading to higher bond issuance. Finland will conduct its first bond auction of the year, selling up to 1.5 billion euros in April 2030- and September 2034-dated bonds on Tuesday. However, it has already issued 3 billion euros in 2045-dated bonds in a syndicated deal in January. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0620 GMT - JGBs fall in price terms during the afternoon Tokyo session, dragged by weak demand at today's auction of 20-year domestic government bonds. The 20-year yield rose as much as 6 bps to 2.065%, the highest intraday level since April 2011, following the announcement of the auction results. The tail, a measure of demand that subtracts the average and lowest accepted prices, at today's auction was 0.55 yen, the most since December 2023. A bigger tail implies weaker demand. The JGB 20-year yield is last 5.5 bps higher at 2.060%; the 10-year yield is up 4.5 bps at 1.430%, the highest intraday level since November 2009. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0615 GMT - Xi Jinping's meeting with Chinese tech leaders provided a clear signal from top leadership of support for private enterprises and entrepreneurs, Goldman Sachs analysts write in a note. The Chinese leader met with business leaders at a Monday meeting, where Xi re-emphasized the importance of the private sector to the economy. That said, the implementation of more demand-side stimulus and more market-friendly policy reforms remain key to rebuilding confidence, GS says. Despite rounds of policy easing, private-sector sentiment remains soft, reflecting the scarring effect of Beijing's zero-Covid policy over 2020-2022 and sector-level regulatory clampdowns in previous years, the bank adds. The March Two Sessions meetings are the next key event to watch, GS says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0524 GMT - The outlook for consumption in China remains cautious despite the encouraging data from the Lunar New Year period, says Alicia Garcia Herrero at Natixis CIB. The LNY began on a positive note, while challenges, such as deflation and a slowdown in exports, stemming from U.S. tariffs, present risks to the growth outlook, the chief economist for Asia Pacific says. Deflationary pressures are evident, even in earlier bright spots, she adds. She points to the decline in the average bank transaction value from 417 yuan to 345 yuan, despite an overall uptick in the volume of bank transactions. The deflationary environment echoes weak investment which doesn't bode well for economic growth, she says. Fiscal announcements during the coming Two Sessions and the PBOC's stance regarding potential interest rate cuts will be critical factors to monitor, she adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0523 GMT - The RBA statement should back prospects for a shallow easing cycle and anchor the Australian dollar against its U.S counterpart in the near term, Westpac's Richard Franulovich says in a research report. The currency pair is likely to continue to target higher levels over a one-month horizon, owing to several factors, the head of the FX Strategy Group says. These factors include ongoing AUD demand from the mining sector for dividend payments and possible policy upside surprises from China's National People's Congress meeting in March, Franulovich adds. Westpac doesn't expect AUD/USD to trade materially below the 0.6300 level. AUD/USD is 0.1% lower at 0.6352. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0458 GMT - The Reserve Bank of Australia is unlikely to follow up with another rate cut until July, says Harry Murphy Cruise at Moody's Analytics. Tuesday's rate cut was widely expected but not a clear-cut one, the economist says. Australia's tight labor market, a flurry of spending at end-2024 offered reasons to hold, while imminent pre-election spending promises could signal bad news for inflation, he says. With global uncertainty and China's slowing economy also potential growth headwinds, it'd be helpful to keep space to cut to spur demand. The RBA's accompanying statement was as hawkish as possible while still announcing a cut, with inflation risk dominating the text, Cruise notes. Reading between the lines the message seems to be: "don't expect more easing any time soon." (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0432 GMT - USD/PHP will likely climb this year as the U.S. policy agenda supports the dollar and weaker external balances in the Philippines weigh on the peso, HSBC strategists say. But due to the central bank's efforts to prevent excessive PHP weakness, plus the Philippines's low exposure to tariff risks, they don't see the pair--last at 58.2--breaking sustainably above 59 in 1H. BSP's short-term preference for FX stability seems to be overriding a medium-term pivot to being less interventionist, Joey Chew and others write. But while PHP may outperform peers with higher exposure to tariff risks and less central bank defense, HSBC says this isn't a reason to chase USD/PHP lower, as BSP may want to curb unwanted strength. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0424 GMT - The Reserve Bank of Australia cited inflation progress in its decision to cut the official cash rate for the first time since 2020 but otherwise cast the decision in mostly hawkish leaning language, cautioning that labor market momentum is strong and explicitly calling out the risks associated with cutting too quickly, says Damien McColough, head of Australian interest rate strategy at Westpac. The RBA said disinflation could stall, and inflation would settle above the midpoint of the target range, he notes. "This does not read like a bank that is primed to deliver back-to-back cuts. If anything they're sitting back and awaiting more conviction," McColough adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0425 GMT - The Reserve Bank of Australia accompanied the start of its easing cycle with a signal that any further withdrawal of monetary restriction will be gradual, Capital Economics' Abhijit Surya says.Recent data has given the RBA board greater confidence that inflation is coming down, but it struck some hawkish tones, the economist writes. The board acknowledged that labor market data have been unexpectedly strong and said some upward pressures on inflation are likely to persist medium term. It noted that if the cash rate evolves in line with its forecasts underlying inflation would remain a bit above the mid-point of the 2%-3% target from late 2025 onward, he adds. That backs CE's view that the easing cycle will prove short-lived, with only two more rate cuts in the pipeline. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0415 GMT - The RBA cautiously cut interest rates Tuesday, noting that inflation risks remain two-sided, but it also spent time highlighting the uncertainties implicit in the storm of policy changes that have been announced by the Trump White House. It said "geopolitical and policy uncertainties are pronounced" and "may themselves bear down on activity in many countries if households and firms delay expenditures pending greater clarity on the outlook." The comments clear the path for the RBA to cut again should the global growth outlook sour suddenly. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Ling Yun Industrial Ties Up With Haomei New Materials to Set Up Moroccan Venture
data.description:
Ling Yun Industrial partnered with aluminum profile manufacturer Guangdong Haomei New Materials to establish a 100 million yuan joint venture in Morocco, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Tuesday.

The venture, to be named Morocco Esther Automotive Industry, will serve as a production base for European and North African markets for new energy batteries, auto body structural parts and high-strength rolled parts for automobile giants such as BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Ford and Volkswagen.

The Chinese automobile components manufacturer will own 51% of the venture through its subsidiary, Aster Automobile Holdings, while Haomei New Materials will own 49% after investing 51 million yuan and 49 million yuan, respectively.

data.title: Guangdong Orient Zirconic Ind Sci & Tech Cancels Wholly Owned Subsidiary
data.description:
Guangdong Orient Zirconic Ind Sci & Tech recently canceled its wholly owned subsidiary, Shantou Dongzi Technical Services, according to a Monday filing with the Shenzhen Bourse.

The company has obtained approval for cancellation of registration from the Shantou Longhu District Market Supervision and Administration Bureau, the filing said.

Accordingly, the company has completed the cancellation registration procedures.

data.title: Silver Supported by Haven Demand
data.description:
Silver prices held steady around $32.4 per ounce on Tuesday, remaining in a sideways trading range for the past two weeks as global trade uncertainties continued to drive safe-haven demand for precious metals.

US President Donald Trump recently announced plans to impose reciprocal tariffs on countries taxing US imports, raising worries over a wider trade war.

Meanwhile, investors kept an eye on US-led efforts to resolve the war in Ukraine, which could reduce demand for safe-haven assets like silver.

Last week, silver reached a three-month high as strong industrial demand, particularly from the electrification and manufacturing sectors, supported prices.

Recent data revealed that China added 357 gigawatts of solar and wind power in 2024, a key driver of industrial silver usage.

data.title: Samvardhana Motherson International's Growth Seen Supported by Tailwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Samvardhana Motherson International's growth may be supported by some tailwinds, Nomura analysts say in a research report as they maintain the stock's buy rating. Availability of Japanese technology along with acquisitions will be leveraged worldwide, based on commentary from the automotive components manufacturer's management. Also, management anticipates very strong ramp-up in the Indian company's consumer electronics plant from 4Q FY 2025, with two larger plants coming online in FY 2026-2027, the analysts note. However, Nomura cuts its target price-to-earnings ratio for the company to 18x from 22x to reflect risks from global uncertainty over tariffs affecting demand. It lowers the stock's target price to INR155.00 from INR209.00. Shares are 1.6% higher at INR126.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Increased revenue and profit growth
data.description:
Increased revenue and profit growth

PR Newswire

STOCKHOLM, Feb. 18, 2025

Year-end report January-December 2024

STOCKHOLM, Feb. 18, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Truecaller, the leading global platform for verifying contacts and blocking unwanted communication, is proud to report an increase in net sales with 23% to SEK 522.8 million (424.7). Net sales reached an all-time high and all income streams contributed to the growth. EBITDA increased with 19% to SEK 201.1 million (168.5) and the EBITDA margin was 38.5% (39.7%). EBITDA excluding costs for incentive programs increased with 36% and the margin increased to 44.3% (40.1%). Net profit increased with 29% and earnings per share increased with 33%. During the quarter average number of daily active users grew with 17 million which was an all-time high user growth in absolute terms.

Extract from CEO Word:

"Net sales increased by 23% in the fourth quarter compared to the same quarter last year, reaching 522 (425) million SEK. All three revenue streams contributed to this growth. Our recurring revenues, from Truecaller for Business (TfB) and Truecaller Premium subscriptions, increased by 42% and now account for 29% (25%) of our total revenue. Advertising revenue continued to grow for the second quarter in a row, now by 17% compared to the same quarter last year.

EBITDA increased by 19% to 201 (169) million SEK, despite larger investments in user growth and increased costs for incentive programs related to a higher share price. EBITDA excluding incentive program costs increased by 36%. The EBITDA margin was 38.5% (39.7%) including incentive program costs. Earnings per share increased by 33% to 0.44 (0.33) SEK per share.

Our user base continued to show strong growth, with 16 million new monthly active users and 17 million new daily active users during the quarter. The average number of monthly active users reached 429 million, an increase of 55 million in one year, corresponding to a growth of 15%. Growth remains broad-based, occurring across many countries and regions. We are rapidly approaching half a billion users, and we have never attracted new users as fast as we did in 2024.

The strategic decisions we have made regarding our Premium offerings are now yielding strong results, with subscription revenue growing by 39% to 78 (56) million SEK. The growth compared to Q3 2024 was 15%. We see strong growth across all regions, increased conversion rates, and higher average revenue per subscriber. Subscription revenue is growing with good pace on both Android and iOS and in all regions with the fastest growth occurring in regions outside India and MEA. In late January, we launched our new iOS product, which will primarily be a premium offering. With users finally getting the full Truecaller experience on iPhones, our ambition is to increase the growth of iOS-subscribers. The new offer should gradually start to have a revenue impact from the second quarter of 2025. Already today, subscription revenues from iOS users is 43% of total subscription revenue.

It is with great humility that I have taken on the role of CEO following the decision of Truecaller's co-founders and my mentors, Alan and Nami, to step down from their operational roles after 15 years. Alan and Nami and the rest of the company have built a great foundation for future growth. As usage and relevance of mobile communication continues to grow, it brings with it immense opportunities for Truecaller to make communication safer, trusted and efficient. We will continue to execute our well-laid-out long-term strategy and back it with Truecaller's agile startup culture, profitable growth and a strong balance sheet. Our conviction is that Truecaller has a much larger role to play in the growing field of mobile communication, including safeguarding people and businesses from the globally growing threat of digital scams and fraud," says Rishit Jhunjhunwala CEO of Truecaller.

October-December 2024 (Q4)

Comparative figures refer to October-December 2023

  • Net sales increased by 23 percent to SEK 522.8m (424.7).
  • EBITDA increased by 19% to SEK 201.1m (168.5), corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 38.5 (39.7) percent. Excluding the costs of incentive programs, EBITDA would have been SEK 231.4m (170.5), equivalent to an EBITDA margin of 44.3 (40.1) percent.
  • Profit after tax increased by 29% to SEK 150.4m (116.2).
  • Basic earnings per share was SEK 0.44 (0.33) and diluted earnings per share were SEK 0.44 (0.33).
  • The average number of active users (MAU) increased by 55.4 million to approximately 429.4 million (374.0).
  • Net sales increased by 20 percent in India, by 43 percent in the Middle East and Africa and by 24 percent in the rest of the world.

January-December 2024

Comparative figures refer to January-December 2023

  • Net sales increased by 8 percent to SEK 1863.2m (1728.9).
  • EBITDA amounted to SEK 684.2m (702.9), corresponding to an EBITDA margin of 36.7 (40.7) percent. Excluding the costs of incentive programs, EBITDA would have been SEK 758.0m (742.7), equivalent to an EBITDA margin of 40.7 (43.0) percent.
  • Profit after tax amounted to SEK 524.3m (536.3).
  • Basic earnings per share were SEK 1.51 (1.49) and diluted earnings per share were SEK 1.51 (1.49).
  • The average number of active users (MAU) increased by 45.3 million to approximately 405.9 million (360.5).
  • Net sales increased by 3 percent in India, with 22 percent in the Middle East and Africa and by 23 percent in the rest of the world.

Presentation of the report

Rishit Jhunjhunwala, CEO and Odd Bolin, CFO presents the report and answers questions in a webcast and conference call on the 18(th) of February at 13.00 CET. The presentation will be held in English.

If you wish to participate via webcast, please use the link below.

https://truecaller.events.inderes.com/q4-report-2024

If you wish to participate via teleconference, please register on the link below. After registration you will be provided phone numbers and a conference ID to access the conference. You can ask questions verbally via the teleconference.

https://conference.inderes.com/teleconference/?id=50050364

For more information, please contact:

Odd Bolin, CFO

investors@truecaller.com

Andreas Frid, Head of IR & Communication

+46 705 29 08 00

andreas.frid@truecaller.com

This information is information that Truecaller is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact persons set out above, at the time stated by the Company's news distributor, Cision, at the publication of this press release.

About Truecaller:

Truecaller (TRUE B) is the leading global platform for verifying contacts and blocking unwanted communication. We enable safe and relevant conversations between people and make it efficient for businesses to connect with consumers. Fraud and unwanted communication are endemic to digital economies. especially in emerging markets. We are on a mission to build trust in communication. Truecaller is an essential part of everyday communication for more than 429 million active users. Headquartered in Stockholm, since 2009, we are a co-founder led. entrepreneurial company. with a highly experienced management team. Truecaller is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 8 October 2021. For more information, please visit corporate.truecaller.com

This information was brought to you by Cision http://news.cision.com.

data.title: Eni to Sell Additional 5% Stake in Enilive to KKR for $616 Million
data.description:
(06:35 GMT) *Eni Signs Pact for KKR to Increase Its Stake in Enilive
(06:37 GMT) *Eni to Sell Additional 5% Stake in Enilive to KKR
(06:37 GMT) *Eni: Purchase Will Be Funded Through Additional Investment of EUR587.5M
(06:38 GMT) *Eni: Investment Will Give KKR a 30% Stake in Enilive
(06:39 GMT) *Eni: Transaction Is in Line With October Deal for KKR's Purchase of 25% Enilive Stake
(06:41 GMT) *Eni: Deal Is Based on Same Valuation of EUR11.75B for 100% of Enilive
(06:42 GMT) *Eni: KKR's Additional Investment Is Backed by International Co-investors
(06:42 GMT) *Eni: Closing of Deal for Additional 5% Stake Is Subject to Regulatory Approvals
(06:55 GMT) Eni to Sell Additional 5% Stake in Enilive to KKR for $616 Million

By Adam Whittaker

Eni said it will sell an additional 5% stake in Enilive to private equity house KKR for 587.5 million euros ($616 million).

The Italian integrated energy company said Tuesday that the sale of additional shares will take KKR's stake in Enilive to 30%.

Eni said that Enilive is a mobility-transformation company which offers integrated services and products that are progressively decarbonized by 2050. The business houses its biorefining and biomethane assets, as well as its Enjoy car-sharing business.

The deal is based on a 11.75 billion euro equity value for 100% of Enilive's share capital, Eni said.

The sale is subject to regulatory approvals, Eni said.

In October, KKR purchased 25% of Enilive's share capital and the sale is expected to close by next month, the company said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: DIFC 2024 revenue rises 37% to 1.78 billion dirhams, Dubai media office says
data.description:
Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) revenue for 2024 was at 1.78 billion AED ($485 million), 37% higher than in 2023, the Dubai media office reported on Tuesday.

DIFC's operating profit grew to 1.33 billion AED in 2024, up 55% from 2023, the media office said.

There is increasing competition among Gulf states to develop their financial sectors as part of economic diversification, with the likes of Abu Dhabi, Riyadh and Doha bolstering investment in their own financial centres.

A quick post-COVID economic rebound, neutral political stance, and ease of doing business have boosted the UAE's appeal among businesses and wealthy individuals in recent years amid global economic uncertainties and growing geopolitical risks.

($1 = 3.6729 UAE dirham)

data.title: PharmX Technologies Partners with New Zealand Pharmacy POS Provider
data.description:
PharmX Technologies has partnered with Toniq, a pharmacy point-of-sale and dispensary software provider in New Zealand, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The collaboration aims to streamline pharmacy operations by enabling pharmacies within the Toniq network to integrate with Pharmx's electronic data interchange, Pharmx Gateway, the filing said.

Pharmacies will be able to access real-time inventory ordering, automated e-invoicing, and seamless supplier connectivity via the Pharmx Gateway, the filing added.

Financial impact details of the deal remain undetermined, per the filing.

data.title: Japan Stocks Rise as BOJ Rate Hike Bets Intensify
data.description:
Japan shares closed higher Tuesday, lifted by expectations of a Bank of Japan rate hike as inflation and hawkish policy signals drove bond yields to multi-year highs.

The Nikkei 225 rose 0.39%, or 151.05 points, to end at 39,325.30.

Markets are bringing forward expectations for a 1% BOJ rate hike to January 2026, citing persistent inflation.

Former BOJ official Nobuyasu Atago sees a potential hike as soon as the April 30-May 1 meeting, noting rising bond yields signal markets are pricing at the risk of an earlier move.

On the corporate front, Frontier Real Estate Investment will acquire a 30% stake in Mitsui Shopping Park LaLaport AICHI TOGO for 10.95 billion yen, comprising the land for 5.75 billion yen and buildings for 5.20 billion yen.

The Aichi Prefecture mall, opened in 2020 with around 200 stores, will be delivered on March 26.

Green Earth Institute , Nippon Paper , and Sumitomo Corp have formed Morisora Bio Refinery to produce bioethanol from woody biomass at Nippon Paper's Iwanuma Mill in Miyagi.

The plant aims for 1,000 kL of annual output by 2027 to support Japan's SAF market, with large-scale expansion planned by 2030.

Media Do has agreed to partner with Amutus Corp. to expand eBook wholesale operations, including joint development for the "Mecha Comic" service and potential new labels.

Media Do will also transfer its subsidiary Everystar Co. to Amutus.

data.title: Path for 10-Year U.S. Treasury Yield Seen Upward on Fiscal Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Growing fiscal concerns have the potential to drive U.S. Treasury yields higher, DZ Bank Research's Birgit Henseler says in a note. The trend for higher yields over a 12-month period could also be strengthened by the debate about tax breaks, the analyst says. "By the autumn at the latest, the debate about tax cuts and an associated expansion of the budget deficit could therefore gain momentum," she says. DZ Bank Research forecasts the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.50% in three months, which is largely in line with the current level. DZ Bank Research expects the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.70% in six months and at 5.00% both at the end of the year and also in 12 months. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Karnov Group BoD Proposes No Dividend
data.description:
Karnov Group AB (publ) :

  • BOARD OF DIRECTORS PROPOSES NO DIVIDEND

  • OUTLOOK: KARNOV GROUP DOES NOT PROVIDE FINANCIAL FORECASTS

data.title: South Korean Shares Gain for Sixth Session, Led by Samsung, Defense Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: South Korean shares gained for a sixth straight session, with the benchmark Kospi closing 0.6% higher at 2626.81. Foreign and institutional investors were net buyers. Index heavyweight Samsung Electronics rose 1.6% following its announcement to cancel around KRW3 trillion of shares. Howitzer and aircraft-engine maker Hanwha Aerospace climbed 11%, extending solid gains after showcasing its long-range surface-to-air missile system at this week's exhibition in Abu Dhabi. Most local defense stocks were higher, with guided missile manufacturer LIG Nex1 rising 9.1%. USD/KRW settled 0.1% higher at 1,443.70 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was down 0.3 bps at 2.866%.(kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Basilea Pharmaceutica Posts Higher FY24 Net Profit, Total Revenue
data.description:
Basilea Pharmaceutica said Tuesday its net profit and total revenue for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 77.6 million francs, compared with 10.5 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 5.83 francs from 0.86 franc.

The Swiss specialty biopharmaceutical company's total revenue was 208.5 million francs, compared with 157.6 million francs a year ago.

For 2025, the company expects a total revenue of 220 million francs.

data.title: DCX Systems Bags INR245.1 Million Orders for Cable and Wire Harness Assemblies
data.description: DCX Systems secured orders worth 245.1 million Indian rupees for the supply of cable and wire harness assemblies, according to an Indian bourse filing on Monday.

data.title: Kinsus Interconnect Technology Logs NT$49 Million Profit in 2024
data.description:
Kinsus Interconnect Technology booked an attributable profit of NT$48.9 million, or NT$0.11 per share for 2024.

The tech firm did not provide comparable year-ago figures.

Operating revenue accumulated stood at NT$30.5 billion, according to a Monday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Burjeel Buys Majority Stake in Dubai's Advanced Care Oncology Center
data.description:
Burjeel Holdings (ADX:BURJEEL) acquired an 80% stake in Dubai-based Advanced Care Oncology Center, or ACOC, for 92 million Emirati dirhams on a cash- and debt-free basis.

The healthcare provider said Tuesday it plans to build an ACOC-branded network for cancer care delivery in underserved markets across the Gulf region and drive medical research and innovation.

Burjeel has an option to acquire the remaining 20% stake in ACOC.

data.title: Eco World Development Unit to Sell Land in Malaysia for MYR694 Million
data.description:
Eco World Development Group unit EcoWorld Malaysia agreed to sell freehold industrial land in Iskandar Malaysia for 694 million ringgit in cash.

The land, measuring about 138.532 acres will be sold to Microsoft Payments (Malaysia), according to a Monday Malaysian bourse filing.

The sale is expected to be completed in the first half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

data.title: Arms stocks soar ahead of Ukraine peace talks
data.description:
ARMS STOCKS SOAR AHEAD OF UKRAINE PEACE TALKS

A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Tom Westbrook

Europe's rally in banking and defence stocks extended into Tokyo, where shares in Mitsubishi Heavy Industries popped 3% to trade near record highs.

The focus is on Ukraine and expectations that Europe will ramp up defence spending in the event of any peace deal.

European futures were slightly higher and pointed to a market open around the record peaks hit on Monday, when the defence sector lifted the broader indexes including the pan-European STOXX 600 and Germany's DAX . Arms maker Rheinmetall surged 14%.

U.S. President Donald Trump has arranged bilateral peace talks with Russia, scheduled to begin later on Tuesday in Saudi Arabia. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was willing to send peacekeeping troops to Ukraine though European leaders made no similar pledge at emergency talks in Paris on Monday.

The possibility of an end to fighting has the euro and European stocks well-supported and has taken a little bit of the limelight from tariffs and interest rates.

It's likely to eclipse Germany's ZEW survey, British labour data and second-tier manufacturing figures due in the U.S.

Australia's central bank, as widely expected, began its rate-cutting cycle by lowering interest rates for the first time since 2020.

The bank sounded, however, like it was in no rush for further cuts, and that lent a little support to the Aussie dollar while the Aussie stock market slipped.

It's a bit of a different story across the Tasman Sea, in New Zealand, where a 50 basis-point cut is priced in for Wednesday and more than 100 bps of easing are expected this year. (0#NZDIRPR)

Hong Kong shares hit their highest since October in morning trade and tech stocks marked a three-year high on a wave of enthusiasm following Monday's rare meeting between President Xi Jinping and business leaders.

Earnings will also be in focus with Chinese search giant Baidu reporting later on Tuesday and Alibaba on Thursday. Baidu shares steadied after a selloff in the previous session when founder Robin Li was not spotted at the symposium in Beijing.

U.S. markets return from a public holiday.

Key developments that could influence markets on Tuesday:

Economics: German ZEW survey, UK labour data, U.S. Empire Fed manufacturing

Earnings: Baidu, InterContinental Hotels

*****

Crisil Keeps A+ Rating on Dr. Agarwal's Eye Hospital's Term Loans, Cash Credit; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Crisil affirmed it's A+ rating on Dr. Agarwal's Eye Hospital's term loans and cash credit, according to a Monday disclosure with the BSE.

The rating outlook is stable, according to the disclosure.

data.title: ASX Censures New Zealand Coastal Seafoods for Breaching Listing Rules
data.description:
New Zealand Coastal Seafoods has been censured by the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX) for breaching Listing Rules in connection with its supply agreement with Hong Kong-based Gold Saint International Resources Group, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company failed to disclose related-party connections between its chairman, Peter Chai, and Gold Saint, as well as the deteriorating financial performance of the supply agreement, the filing said.

NZS did not comply with the necessary disclosure requirements, resulting in significant delays and further breaches, the filing added.

ASX also cited NZS' poor history of compliance with periodic reporting and financial forecasts.
